Here's When Each One Actually Works. - DEV Community](https://dev.to/nedcodes/cursor-rules-vs-agent-skills-i-tested-both-heres-when-each-one-actually-works-1ld)<br/><br/># Cursor IDE Agent Skills and Rules (2025-2026)<br/><br/>## Agent Skills Overview<br/>Agent Skills shipped in **Cursor v2.4 (January 2026)** as a way to extend AI agents with specialized capabilities.[1] A skill is a folder containing a `SKILL.md` file with markdown instructions that teach the agent domain-specific tasks.[1][4]<br/><br/>**Key characteristics:**<br/>- **Dynamic loading**: The agent reads skill descriptions to determine relevance, then loads full instructions only when needed[1]<br/>- **Progressive disclosure**: Keeps context window clean by loading only relevant expertise[2]<br/>- **Just-in-time intelligence**: Skills load on demand rather than consuming context permanently[2]<br/><br/>## How Skills Work<br/>The agent follows a three-step flow: it discovers skills by scanning names and descriptions, determines if they're relevant to the current task, then loads the full instructions if applicable.[1] Skills can be manually invoked using the `/` prefix in Chat or Composer.[2]<br/><br/>## Skill File Structure<br/>Skills are stored in `.cursor/skills/`, `.agents/skills/`, or `~/.cursor/skills/` and consist of:[4]<br/>- A `SKILL.md` file with YAML frontmatter (required fields: `name`, `description`)<br/>- Optional `scripts/`, `references/`, and `assets/` directories<br/>- Optional `disable-model-invocation: true` to make skills manual-only<br/><br/>## Rules vs. Skills Distinction<br/>**Rules** (`.cursor/rules/`) are always-on guardrails applied to every interaction, while **Skills** are dynamic capabilities the agent discovers and applies when relevant.[1][2] Rules are better for enforcing consistent standards; skills excel at procedural, multi-step workflows.[5]<br/><br/>The official position: skills and rules are complementary, not competing.[5] Rules aren't deprecated.[5] |

</details>
</tool-use>

{
  "url": "https://cursor.com/docs/context/skills",
  "markdown": "# Agent Skills\n\nAgent Skills is an open standard for extending AI agents with specialized capabilities. Skills package domain-specific knowledge and workflows that agents can use to perform specific tasks.\n\n## What are skills?\n\nA skill is a portable, version-controlled package that teaches agents how to perform domain-specific tasks. Skills can include scripts, templates, and references that agents may act on using their tools.\n\n### Portable\n\nSkills work across any agent that supports the Agent Skills standard.\n\n### Version-controlled\n\nSkills are stored as files and can be tracked in your repository, or installed via GitHub repository links.\n\n### Actionable\n\nSkills can include scripts, templates, and references that agents act on using their tools.\n\n### Progressive\n\nSkills load resources on demand, keeping context usage efficient.\n\n## How skills work\n\nWhen Cursor starts, it automatically discovers skills from skill directories and makes them available to Agent. The agent is presented with available skills and decides when they are relevant based on context.\n\nSkills can also be manually invoked by typing &#96;/&#96; in Agent chat and searching for the skill name.\n\n## Skill directories\n\nSkills are automatically loaded from these locations:\n\n| Location | Scope |\n| --- | --- |\n| &#96;.agents/skills/&#96; | Project-level |\n| &#96;.cursor/skills/&#96; | Project-level |\n| &#96;~/.cursor/skills/&#96; | User-level (global) |\n\nFor compatibility, Cursor also loads skills from Claude and Codex directories: &#96;.claude/skills/&#96;, &#96;.codex/skills/&#96;, &#96;~/.claude/skills/&#96;, and &#96;~/.codex/skills/&#96;.\n\nEach skill should be a folder containing a &#96;SKILL.md&#96; file:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\n.agents/\n└── skills/\n    └── my-skill/\n        └── SKILL.md\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSkills can also include optional directories for scripts, references, and assets:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\n.agents/\n└── skills/\n    └── deploy-app/\n        ├── SKILL.md\n        ├── scripts/\n        │   ├── deploy.sh\n        │   └── validate.py\n        ├── references/\n        │   └── REFERENCE.md\n        └── assets/\n            └── config-template.json\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## SKILL.md file format\n\nEach skill is defined in a &#96;SKILL.md&#96; file with YAML frontmatter:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;markdown\n---\nname: my-skill\ndescription: Short description of what this skill does and when to use it.\n---\n\n# My Skill\n\nDetailed instructions for the agent.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- Use this skill when...\n- This skill is helpful for...\n\n## Instructions\n\n- Step-by-step guidance for the agent\n- Domain-specific conventions\n- Best practices and patterns\n- Use the ask questions tool if you need to clarify requirements with the user\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Frontmatter fields\n\n| Field | Required | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| &#96;name&#96; | Yes | Skill identifier. Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only. Must match the parent folder name. |\n| &#96;description&#96; | Yes | Describes what the skill does and when to use it. Used by the agent to determine relevance. |\n| &#96;license&#96; | No | License name or reference to a bundled license file. |\n| &#96;compatibility&#96; | No | Environment requirements (system packages, network access, etc.). |\n| &#96;metadata&#96; | No | Arbitrary key-value mapping for additional metadata. |\n| &#96;disable-model-invocation&#96; | No | When &#96;true&#96;, the skill is only included when explicitly invoked via &#96;/skill-name&#96;. The agent will not automatically apply it based on context. |\n\n## Disabling automatic invocation\n\nBy default, skills are automatically applied when the agent determines they are relevant. Set &#96;disable-model-invocation: true&#96; to make a skill behave like a traditional slash command, where it is only included in context when you explicitly type &#96;/skill-name&#96; in chat.\n\n## Including scripts in skills\n\nSkills can include a &#96;scripts/&#96; directory containing executable code that agents can run. Reference scripts in your &#96;SKILL.md&#96; using relative paths from the skill root.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;markdown\n---\nname: deploy-app\ndescription: Deploy the application to staging or production environments. Use when deploying code or when the user mentions deployment, releases, or environments.\n---\n\n# Deploy App\n\nDeploy the application using the provided scripts.\n\n## Usage\n\nRun the deployment script: &#96;scripts/deploy.sh &lt;environment&gt;&#96;\n\nWhere &#96;&lt;environment&gt;&#96; is either &#96;staging&#96; or &#96;production&#96;.\n\n## Pre-deployment Validation\n\nBefore deploying, run the validation script: &#96;python scripts/validate.py&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe agent reads these instructions and executes the referenced scripts when the skill is invoked. Scripts can be written in any language—Bash, Python, JavaScript, or any other executable format supported by the agent implementation.\n\nScripts should be self-contained, include helpful error messages, and handle edge cases gracefully.\n\n## Optional directories\n\nSkills support these optional directories:\n\n| Directory | Purpose |\n| --- | --- |\n| &#96;scripts/&#96; | Executable code that agents can run |\n| &#96;references/&#96; | Additional documentation loaded on demand |\n| &#96;assets/&#96; | Static resources like templates, images, or data files |\n\nKeep your main &#96;SKILL.md&#96; focused and move detailed reference material to separate files. This keeps context usage efficient since agents load resources progressively—only when needed.\n\n## Viewing skills\n\nTo view discovered skills:\n\n1. Skills appear in the Agent Decides section\n2. Navigate to Rules\n3. Open Cursor Settings (Cmd+Shift+J on Mac, Ctrl+Shift+J on Windows/Linux)\n\n## Installing skills from GitHub\n\nYou can import skills from GitHub repositories:\n\n1. Enter the GitHub repository URL\n2. Select Remote Rule (Github)\n3. In the Project Rules section, click Add Rule\n4. Open Cursor Settings → Rules\n\n## Migrating rules and commands to skills\n\nCursor includes a built-in &#96;/migrate-to-skills&#96; skill in 2.4 that helps you convert existing dynamic rules and slash commands to skills.\n\nThe migration skill converts:\n\n- Slash commands: Both user-level and workspace-level commands are converted to skills with &#96;disable-model-invocation: true&#96;, preserving their explicit invocation behavior.\n- Dynamic rules: Rules that use the \"Apply Intelligently\" configuration—rules with &#96;alwaysApply: false&#96; (or undefined) and no &#96;globs&#96; patterns defined. These are converted to standard skills.\n\nTo migrate:\n\n1. Review the generated skills in &#96;.cursor/skills/&#96;\n2. The agent will identify eligible rules and commands and convert them to skills\n3. Type &#96;/migrate-to-skills&#96; in Agent chat\n\nRules with &#96;alwaysApply: true&#96; or specific &#96;globs&#96; patterns are not migrated, as they have explicit triggering conditions that differ from skill behavior. User rules are also not migrated since they are not stored on the file system.\n\n## Learn more\n\nAgent Skills is an open standard. Learn more at [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io/).\n\n---\n\n## Sitemap\n\n[Overview of all docs pages](https://cursor.com/llms.txt)"
}

[Implementing a secure sandbox for local agents · Cursor](https://cursor.com/blog/agent-sandboxing)<br/>3. [sandbox.json Reference \| Cursor Docs](https://cursor.com/docs/reference/sandbox)<br/>4. [Security & Network \| Cursor Docs](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/security)<br/>5. [Terminal \| Cursor Docs](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/tools/terminal)<br/><br/># Cursor Agent Skill Sandbox Isolation Security (2026)<br/><br/>## Sandbox Architecture<br/><br/>Cursor has implemented agent sandboxing across macOS, Linux, and Windows to balance security with usability.[2] The system uses platform-specific technologies: macOS uses Apple's Seatbelt (sandbox-exec), Linux uses Landlock and seccomp, and Windows runs the Linux sandbox inside WSL2.[1][2]<br/><br/>## Key Security Benefits<br/><br/>Sandboxed agents reduce interruptions by 40% compared to unsandboxed versions by allowing agents to run freely within controlled environments and only request approval when stepping outside the sandbox—primarily for internet access.[1][2] This reduces "approval fatigue" that undermines security effectiveness.<br/><br/>## Access Control<br/><br/>The sandbox provides granular file and network access controls:[5]<br/>- **File access**: Read-only to filesystem; read-write to workspace directories<br/>- **Network access**: Blocked by default, configurable via `sandbox.json`<br/>- **Protected paths**: Always write-protected include `.git/config`, `.vscode/`, and `.cursorignore`<br/><br/>Writable `.cursor` subdirectories include `rules/`, `commands/`, `worktrees/`, `skills/`, and `agents/`.[3]<br/><br/>## Configuration & Policies<br/><br/>Users configure sandbox behavior through `sandbox.json` with workspace-level and per-user settings.[3] Enterprise team-admin policies and hardcoded security rules layer on top and cannot be weakened.[3] Network policies support exact domains, wildcards, and CIDR notation, with RFC 1918 private addresses blocked by default to prevent SSRF attacks.[3]<br/><br/>## Enterprise Support<br/><br/>NVIDIA is confirmed as an enterprise customer fully onboarded onto Cursor's sandbox system.[1] |

</details>

{
  "url": "https://cursor.com/docs/reference/sandbox",
  "markdown": "# sandbox.json reference\n\nConfigure [sandbox](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/tools/terminal.md#sandbox) behavior with a &#96;sandbox.json&#96; file to control network access, filesystem paths, and more.\n\n## File locations\n\nPlace &#96;sandbox.json&#96; in either or both of these locations:\n\n| Location | Scope | Priority |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| &#96;~/.cursor/sandbox.json&#96; | All workspaces (per-user) | Lower |\n| &#96; /.cursor/sandbox.json&#96; | Single workspace (per-repo) | Higher |\n\nBoth files are optional. When both exist, they are merged with per-repo settings taking priority. Enterprise team-admin policies and Cursor's hardcoded security rules layer on top and cannot be weakened by either file.\n\n## Top-level fields\n\nAll fields are optional. Missing fields use the defaults shown below.\n\n| Field | Type | Default | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| &#96;type&#96; | string | &#96;\"workspace_readwrite\"&#96; | Sandbox mode. &#96;\"workspace_readwrite\"&#96; gives read/write access in the workspace. &#96;\"workspace_readonly\"&#96; restricts to read-only. &#96;\"insecure_none\"&#96; disables the sandbox entirely. |\n| &#96;additionalReadwritePaths&#96; | &#96;string[]&#96; | &#96;[]&#96; | Extra paths the agent can read and write. Only applies when &#96;type&#96; is &#96;\"workspace_readwrite\"&#96;. |\n| &#96;additionalReadonlyPaths&#96; | &#96;string[]&#96; | &#96;[]&#96; | Extra paths the agent can read. |\n| &#96;disableTmpWrite&#96; | &#96;boolean&#96; | &#96;false&#96; | When &#96;true&#96;, removes default write access to &#96;/tmp&#96; and system temp directories. |\n| &#96;enableSharedBuildCache&#96; | &#96;boolean&#96; | &#96;false&#96; | Redirects build-tool caches (npm, cargo, pip, etc.) to a shared tmpdir so sandboxed and unsandboxed commands share the same caches. |\n\n## &#96;networkPolicy&#96; object\n\n| Field | Type | Default | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| &#96;default&#96; | &#96;\"allow\"&#96; | &#96;\"deny\"&#96; | &#96;\"deny\"&#96; | Action when no allow/deny rule matches. |\n| &#96;allow&#96; | &#96;string[]&#96; | &#96;[]&#96; | Patterns to allow. Supports exact domains, wildcards, and CIDR notation. |\n| &#96;deny&#96; | &#96;string[]&#96; | &#96;[]&#96; | Patterns to deny. Highest priority; always blocks, even if a pattern also appears in &#96;allow&#96;. |\n\n## Network pattern syntax\n\nThe &#96;allow&#96; and &#96;deny&#96; arrays accept three pattern formats:\n\n| Format | Example | Matches |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| Exact domain | &#96;\"registry.npmjs.org\"&#96; | That exact host |\n| Wildcard | &#96;\"*.example.com\"&#96; | Any subdomain of &#96;example.com&#96;, including &#96;example.com&#96; itself |\n| CIDR | &#96;\"10.0.0.0/8\"&#96; | Any IP in that range |\n\nKey rules:\n\n- URL paths are ignored; matching is domain/IP only.\n- IPv6 private addresses (&#96;::1&#96;, &#96;fe80::/10&#96;, &#96;fc00::/7&#96;) are also blocked.\n- Private/RFC 1918 addresses (&#96;10.x&#96;, &#96;172.16.x&#96;, &#96;192.168.x&#96;, &#96;127.x&#96;) and cloud metadata endpoints (&#96;169.254.169.254&#96;) are blocked by default to prevent SSRF.\n- Deny always beats allow. If a host matches both lists, it is blocked.\n\n## How policies merge\n\nWhen multiple policy sources exist, they merge in priority order:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\nper-user  &lt;  per-repo  &lt;  team-admin  &lt;  hardcoded\n(lowest)                                (highest)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nMerge rules:\n\n- Restrictive booleans (&#96;disableTmpWrite&#96;, &#96;networkPolicyStrict&#96;): &#96;true&#96; wins.\n- &#96;networkPolicy.default&#96;: &#96;\"deny\"&#96; wins over &#96;\"allow\"&#96;.\n- Network deny lists: always unioned.\n- Network allow lists: unioned, unless a team-admin allowlist is present (which replaces the union).\n- Paths (&#96;additionalReadwritePaths&#96;, &#96;additionalReadonlyPaths&#96;): unioned across all sources.\n\n## Protected paths\n\nCertain paths are always write-protected, regardless of your &#96;sandbox.json&#96; configuration:\n\n- &#96;.cursorignore&#96;\n- &#96;.git/hooks/**&#96;, &#96;.git/config&#96;, &#96;.git/info/attributes&#96;\n- &#96;.code-workspace&#96;\n- &#96;.vscode/**&#96;\n- &#96;.claude/*.json&#96;, &#96;.claude/**/*.json&#96;\n- &#96;.cursor/*.json&#96;, &#96;.cursor/**/*.json&#96;, &#96;.cursor/.workspace-trusted&#96;\n\nThe following &#96;.cursor&#96; subdirectories are writable: &#96;rules/&#96;, &#96;commands/&#96;, &#96;worktrees/&#96;, &#96;skills/&#96;, &#96;agents/&#96;.\n\nSSL certificate paths and &#96;~/.ssh&#96; are always readable.\n\n## Examples\n\n### Allow specific domains\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"networkPolicy\": {\n    \"default\": \"deny\",\n    \"allow\": [\n      \"registry.npmjs.org\",\n      \"pypi.org\",\n      \"*.githubusercontent.com\"\n    ]\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nNetwork traffic is denied by default. Only the listed domains are reachable.\n\n### Allow all network\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"networkPolicy\": {\n    \"default\": \"allow\"\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAll outbound network traffic is permitted inside the sandbox.\n\n### Full-stack web project\n\nA project where the agent needs to install packages, pull container images, access a database on the local network, and read a shared design-tokens repo:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"networkPolicy\": {\n    \"default\": \"deny\",\n    \"allow\": [\n      \"registry.npmjs.org\",\n      \"registry.yarnpkg.com\",\n      \"pypi.org\",\n      \"files.pythonhosted.org\",\n      \"*.docker.io\",\n      \"ghcr.io\",\n      \"*.googleapis.com\"\n    ],\n    \"deny\": [\n      \"*.internal.corp.example.com\"\n    ]\n  },\n  \"additionalReadwritePaths\": [\n    \"/home/me/.docker\"\n  ],\n  \"additionalReadonlyPaths\": [\n    \"/opt/shared/design-tokens\"\n  ],\n  \"enableSharedBuildCache\": true\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis configuration lets the agent:\n\n- Share npm/pip/cargo caches between sandboxed and unsandboxed runs.\n- Read (but not modify) a shared design-tokens directory.\n- Write to &#96;~/.docker&#96; for container operations.\n- Block access to internal corporate services.\n- Hit Google Cloud APIs.\n- Install npm/pip packages and pull Docker images.\n\n---\n\n## Sitemap\n\n[Overview of all docs pages](https://cursor.com/llms.txt)"
}
```

---

## 1. Cursor 技术文档（已读）

已根据 [cursor.com/docs](https://cursor.com/docs/context/skills) 整理技术摘要，并写入：

`数字分身/AI产出/系统搭建/[产出] Cursor-Skills技术摘要-2026-03-14.md`

要点：
- **Agent Skills**：`SKILL.md` + YAML frontmatter，目录 `.cursor/skills/`、`.agents/skills/`
- **沙盒**：`sandbox.json` 控制网络、文件路径
- **Rules vs Skills**：Rules 常驻，Skills 按需或 `/` 触发

---

## 2. SK 架构与新增 SK

| 新增 | 路径 | 作用 |
|------|------|------|
| **cursor-features** | `.cursor/skills/cursor-features/` | 跟踪 Cursor 文档与功能 |
| **skill-architect-matcher** | `.cursor/skills/skill-architect-matcher/` | 在 ClawHub + skills.sh 中匹配 SK，并强制走 skill-vetter |

与数字分身 SK（SK-00~10）的关系：
- 数字分身 SK：在 `Skills模板/` 中，管「懂你、帮你决策」
- Cursor Skills：管「怎么用 Cursor、怎么连外部 SK」

---

## 3. 全球 SK 平台与匹配流程

| 平台 | 规模 | 工具 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|------|
| **ClawHub** | 3286+ skills | `clawhub search` / `clawhub install` | OpenClaw 官方，语义搜索 |
| **skills.sh** | Cursor 生态 | `npx skills find` / `npx skills add` | Vercel、ComposioHQ 等 |

**强制安装流程**：需求 → 搜索 → **skill-vetter 审查** → 沙盒环境安装 → 验证  

ClawHavoc 后需谨慎：优先高星、高下载、可信作者。

---

## 4. 沙盒

已创建 `.cursor/sandbox.json`：

- 网络默认 `deny`，仅放行：npm / pypi / GitHub / ClawHub / openclaw 等
- 受保护路径：`.git/config`、`.vscode/**`、`.cursor/*.json` 等
- 新安装或自建 SK 若带 `scripts/`，将在沙盒中执行

---

## 使用方式

- **查 Cursor 功能**：直接读 `cursor-features` 或技术摘要
- **匹配外部 SK**：用 `skill-architect-matcher`，并按其中流程执行
- **安装前**：必须走 `skill-vetter`，避免恶意 SK

---

#### 3. 下一步指令

- **去执行**：下次要装外部 SK 时，按 `skill-architect-matcher` 流程：搜索 → vetter → 沙盒安装

## 1. SK-01 监控系统的意义

**跨会话的信息提炼与知识库驱动**。

```
SK-00（或 SpecStory）→ 记：全量记录
        ↓
SK-01 → 扫：启动时读上一轮记录，识别需要落盘/纠偏的内容
        ↓
输出标记清单 → 指导核心画像、动态状态、事件库等更新
```

**核心作用**：
- **解决「关窗口就丢信息」**：上一轮对话被压缩或关闭后，新开窗口时通过扫描，把应归档的内容拉出来
- **产出结构化标记**：`[画像+]`、`[画像?]`、`[事件+]`、`[人物+]` 等，而不是散乱文本
- **衔接记录层与知识库**：从原始记录里筛出「该写进哪份文件」的信号

与交接备忘的关系：交接备忘是「人工维护的桥」，SK-01 是「自动扫描的桥」——两者都解决跨窗口记忆，SK-01 更偏自动化、结构化。

---

## 2. 把「匹配 SKILLS」定义为 SK-0？

**SK-0 的语义**：若用 SK-0 表示「第 0 层 / 元层 / 治理层」，匹配 SKILLS 在逻辑上确实属于这一层——它管的是「有哪些 SK 可用」，而不是「用哪个 SK 处理当前话题」。

**与现有编号的关系**：

| 编号 | 当前含义 | 层次 |
|------|----------|------|
| SK-00 | 长期记忆（已外部化） | 记录层 |
| SK-01 | 系统监控（启动扫描） | 跨会话提炼 |
| SK-06 | 上帝视角（总控、触发判断） | 元层 |
| **匹配 SKILLS** | 在 ClawHub/GitHub/Claude 等找、审、装 SK | **能力栈治理层** |

**两种做法**：

| 方案 | 做法 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
| A | 不纳入数字分身 SK 编号 | 保持为 Cursor Skill（skill-architect-matcher），和 SK-00~10 分开 |
| B | 纳入并编号为 SK-0 | 在数字分身里新增 SK-0「能力栈治理」，与 SK-06 并列：SK-06 负责「触发谁」，SK-0 负责「扩展谁」 |

**建议**：若希望「匹配 SKILLS」成为数字分身正式能力，用 **SK-0** 是合理的——表示在 SK-00 之上、比业务 SK 更「元」的治理能力；与 SK-00 的「00」区分开，表示不同维度（记录 vs 能力栈）。

要不要正式在 `_INDEX.md` 和 Skills 模板里增加 SK-0 定义？

- **长期应该在 N8N 里完成**：把 `clean_specstory()` 的逻辑迁移到 N8N 的 Code 节点，与从 GitHub 拉取 SpecStory 的流程打通，形成自动化管道。

[Cursor CLI](https://docs.specstory.com/docs/integrations/cursor-cli)<br/><br/># SpecStory Extension for Cursor<br/><br/>The SpecStory extension integrates with Cursor to automatically capture and save AI conversations as searchable Markdown files.[1] Here's what you need to know:<br/><br/>## Installation & Setup<br/>Install the extension directly from Cursor's marketplace (not the VS Code Marketplace) to ensure it works properly.[1] After installation, verify it's working by opening the Command Palette and typing "SpecStory".[1]<br/><br/>## Auto-Save Feature<br/>Every AI conversation in Cursor is automatically saved to `.specstory/history/` in your project as clean Markdown files with timestamps.[1] This preserves your reasoning, tradeoffs, and design decisions alongside your code.[1]<br/><br/>## Key Features<br/>- **Manual saves**: Use `SpecStory: Save AI Chat History` from the Command Palette to cherry-pick conversations[1]<br/>- **Share conversations**: Run `SpecStory: Share AI Chat History` to generate private, anonymous shareable links[1]<br/>- **Derived rules**: SpecStory can extract project-level rules from your chats to improve Cursor's AI suggestions[1]<br/>- **Local-first**: Nothing leaves your machine unless you explicitly share it[1]<br/><br/>## Best Practices<br/>Include `.specstory/history/` in your repository so teammates can review the reasoning behind code changes in pull requests.[1] You can also use manual saves before major changes to preserve conversation context.[1]<br/><br/>The extension works with Cursor v0.43.5 and later.[4] |

已写成 PLAN 分析，并写入 `数字分身/AI产出/系统搭建/[产出] 新窗口接续与会话满备份-PLAN分析-2026-03-14.md`。

---

## 简要结论

### 1. 新窗口接续（省 token）

| 项目 | 内容 |
|------|------|
| 可行性 | 可行：SpecStory 已写入 `.specstory/history/`，按时间取最新即可 |
| 流程 | 用户说「我在新窗口」「接上文」→ SK 读最新 SpecStory（可只读尾部 N 轮）→ 简短总结后问「这次要处理什么？」 |
| 实现 | 在 SK/CLAUDE 中加触发词，并实现「读最新文件」逻辑 |

### 2. 会话满时的自动备份

| 项目 | 内容 |
|------|------|
| Open as Editor | 有该功能（More → Open Chat in Editor），但**无 API**，无法自动化 |
| 自动存对话导出 | 可行：SpecStory 已自动保存，只需对**最新 SpecStory 文件**跑 `export_transcript.py`，写入 `数字分身/对话导出/` |
| 自动命名 | `export_transcript.py` 已支持 `[导出] YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-话题.md` 格式 |

要点：**不能自动触发 Open as Editor**，但**可以自动完成**：读最新 SpecStory → 清洗 → 存到对话导出并按规则命名。

---

## 推荐实施顺序

1. **新窗口接续**：加触发词 + 读最新 SpecStory（或仅尾部）+ 交接备忘  
2. **会话满备份**：提醒话术 + 调用 `export_transcript.py` 处理最新 SpecStory，写入对话导出  
3. **Open as Editor**：写进操作说明，作为人工可选步骤

需要的话，我可以按这个 PLAN 直接改 SK/CLAUDE 和交接备忘。

[Cursor自定义API完全指南：配置、优化与故障排除（2025最新教程） - Cursor IDE 博客](https://www.cursor-ide.com/blog/cursor-custom-api)<br/><br/># Cursor IDE API 2026 官方信息<br/><br/>## 官方 API 概览[1]<br/><br/>Cursor 提供多种企业级 API，用于程序化访问团队数据、AI 驱动编码代理和分析功能：<br/><br/>\| API \| 描述 \| 可用性 \|<br/>\|-----\|------\|--------\|<br/>\| Admin API \| 管理团队成员、设置、使用数据和支出 \| 企业版团队 \|<br/>\| Analytics API \| 提供团队使用、AI 指标、活跃用户数据的洞察 \| 企业版团队 \|<br/>\| AI Code Tracking API \| 在提交与变更层级追踪 AI 生成代码贡献 \| 企业版团队 \|<br/>\| Cloud Agents API \| 创建与管理 AI 驱动的编码代理 \| 测试版（所有套餐） \|<br/><br/>## 身份验证与 API 密钥[1]<br/><br/>所有 Cursor API 使用基本身份验证。API 密钥在团队设置中创建，仅团队管理员可操作。密钥格式为：`key_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`<br/><br/>## 速率限制[1]<br/><br/>- Admin API：20 次请求/分钟（`/teams/user-spend-limit` 端点为 250 次/分钟）<br/>- Analytics API：100 次请求/分钟（团队级），50 次请求/分钟（按用户）<br/>- AI Code Tracking API：20 次请求/分钟<br/>- 超限返回 `429 Too Many Requests` 响应<br/><br/>## 缓存支持[1]<br/><br/>AI Code Tracking API 和 Analytics API 支持使用 ETag 的 HTTP 缓存，304 响应不计入速率限制，缓存时长为 15 分钟。<br/><br/>## 自定义 API Keys 配置[2][4]<br/><br/>用户还可在 Cursor Settings > Models 中配置自定义 API Keys（OpenAI、Anthropic、Google、Azure），实现无限制使用自己的 API 额度，但部分功能（如 Tab 自动补全）可能有限制。 |

</details>
</tool-use>

{
  "url": "https://cursor.com/docs/api",
  "markdown": "# Cursor APIs Overview\n\nCursor provides multiple APIs for programmatic access to your team's data, AI-powered coding agents, and analytics.\n\n## Available APIs\n\n| API | Description | Availability |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| [Admin API](https://cursor.com/docs/account/teams/admin-api.md) | Manage team members, settings, usage data, and spending. Build custom dashboards and monitoring tools. | Enterprise teams |\n| [Analytics API](https://cursor.com/docs/account/teams/analytics-api.md) | Comprehensive insights into team's Cursor usage, AI metrics, active users, and model usage. | Enterprise teams |\n| [AI Code Tracking API](https://cursor.com/docs/account/teams/ai-code-tracking-api.md) | Track AI-generated code contributions at commit and change levels for attribution and analytics. | Enterprise teams |\n| [Cloud Agents API](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/api/endpoints.md) | Programmatically create and manage AI-powered coding agents for automated workflows and code generation. | Beta (All Plans) |\n\n## Authentication\n\nAll Cursor APIs use Basic Authentication.\n\n### Basic Authentication\n\nUse your API key as the username in basic authentication (leave password empty):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ncurl https://api.cursor.com/teams/members \\\n  -u YOUR_API_KEY:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOr set the Authorization header directly:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nAuthorization: Basic {base64_encode('YOUR_API_KEY:')}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Creating API Keys\n\nAPI keys are created from your team settings. Only team administrators can create and manage API keys.\n\n#### Admin API &amp; AI Code Tracking API\n\n1. Copy the generated key immediately - you won't see it again\n2. Give your key a descriptive name (e.g., \"Usage Dashboard Integration\")\n3. Click Create New API Key\n4. Navigate to cursor.com/dashboard → Settings tab → Advanced → Admin API Keys\n\nKey format: &#96;key_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx&#96;\n\n#### Analytics API\n\nGenerate an API key from your [team settings page](https://cursor.com/settings).\n\n#### Cloud Agents API\n\nCreate an API key from [Cursor Dashboard → Integrations](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=integrations).\n\nAPI keys are tied to your organization and viewable by all admins. Keys are unaffected by the original creator's account status.\n\n## Rate Limits\n\nAll APIs implement rate limiting to ensure fair usage and system stability. Rate limits are enforced per team and reset every minute.\n\n### Rate Limits by API\n\n| API | Endpoint Type | Rate Limit |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| Admin API | Most endpoints | 20 requests/minute |\n| Admin API | &#96;/teams/user-spend-limit&#96; | 250 requests/minute |\n| Analytics API | Team-level endpoints | 100 requests/minute |\n| Analytics API | By-user endpoints | 50 requests/minute |\n| AI Code Tracking API | All endpoints | 20 requests/minute per endpoint |\n| Cloud Agents API | All endpoints | Standard rate limiting |\n\n### Rate Limit Response\n\nWhen you exceed the rate limit, you'll receive a &#96;429 Too Many Requests&#96; response:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"error\": \"Too Many Requests\",\n  \"message\": \"Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.\"\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Caching\n\nSeveral APIs support HTTP caching with ETags to reduce bandwidth usage and improve performance.\n\n### Supported APIs\n\n- AI Code Tracking API: Endpoints support HTTP caching\n- Analytics API: All endpoints (both team-level and by-user) support HTTP caching\n\n### How Caching Works\n\n1. 304 Not Modified: If data hasn't changed, you'll receive a &#96;304 Not Modified&#96; response with no body\n2. Subsequent Requests: Include the &#96;ETag&#96; value in an &#96;If-None-Match&#96; header\n3. Response Includes ETag: The API returns an &#96;ETag&#96; header in the response\n4. Initial Request: Make a request to any supported endpoint\n\n### Example\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Initial request\ncurl -X GET \"https://api.cursor.com/analytics/team/dau\" \\\n  -H \"Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY\" \\\n  -D headers.txt\n\n# Response includes: ETag: \"abc123xyz\"\n\n# Subsequent request with ETag\ncurl -X GET \"https://api.cursor.com/analytics/team/dau\" \\\n  -H \"Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY\" \\\n  -H \"If-None-Match: \\\"abc123xyz\\\"\"\n\n# Returns 304 Not Modified if data hasn't changed\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Cache Duration\n\n- Include &#96;If-None-Match&#96; header in subsequent requests to receive &#96;304 Not Modified&#96; when data hasn't changed\n- Responses include an &#96;ETag&#96; header\n- Cache duration: 15 minutes (&#96;Cache-Control: public, max-age=900&#96;)\n\n### Benefits\n\n- Better performance: Especially useful for frequently polled endpoints\n- Rate limit friendly: 304 responses don't count against rate limits\n- Faster responses: Avoids processing unchanged data\n- Reduces bandwidth usage: 304 responses contain no body\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Implement Exponential Backoff\n\nWhen you receive a 429 response, wait before retrying with increasing delays:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nimport time\nimport requests\n\ndef make_request_with_backoff(url, headers, max_retries=5):\n    for attempt in range(max_retries):\n        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)\n        \n        if response.status_code == 429:\n            # Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s\n            wait_time = 2 ** attempt\n            print(f\"Rate limited. Waiting {wait_time}s before retry...\")\n            time.sleep(wait_time)\n            continue\n            \n        return response\n    \n    raise Exception(\"Max retries exceeded\")\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 2. Distribute Requests Over Time\n\nSpread your API calls over time rather than making burst requests:\n\n- Use queuing systems to smooth out traffic spikes\n- Add delays between requests when processing large datasets\n- Schedule batch jobs to run at different intervals\n\n### 3. Leverage Caching\n\nFor Analytics API and AI Code Tracking API:\n\nThese APIs support HTTP caching with ETags. See the [Caching](https://cursor.com/docs/api.md#caching) section above for details on how to use ETags to reduce bandwidth usage and avoid unnecessary requests.\n\nKey benefits:\n\n- Doesn't count against rate limits (for 304 responses)\n- Faster responses when data hasn't changed\n- Reduces bandwidth usage\n\nUse date shortcuts (&#96;7d&#96;, &#96;30d&#96;) instead of timestamps for better caching support in Analytics API.\n\n### 4. Monitor Your Usage\n\nTrack your request patterns to stay within limits:\n\n- Adjust polling intervals based on actual needs\n- Monitor daily/weekly usage trends\n- Set up alerts for 429 responses\n- Log API call timestamps and response codes\n\n### 5. Batch Wisely\n\nFor endpoints with pagination:\n\n- For large data extractions: Use CSV endpoints when available (they stream data efficiently)\n- For Analytics API by-user endpoints: Use &#96;users&#96; parameter to filter specific users\n- Use appropriate page sizes to get more data per request\n\n### 6. Poll at Appropriate Intervals\n\nDon't over-poll endpoints that update infrequently:\n\n- AI Code Tracking API: Data is ingested in near real-time but polling every few minutes is sufficient\n- Analytics API: Use date shortcuts (&#96;7d&#96;, &#96;30d&#96;) for better caching support\n- Admin API &#96;/teams/filtered-usage-events&#96;: Poll at most once per hour (data aggregated hourly)\n- Admin API &#96;/teams/daily-usage-data&#96;: Poll at most once per hour (data aggregated hourly)\n\n### 7. Handle Errors Gracefully\n\nImplement proper error handling for all API calls:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;javascript\nasync function fetchAnalytics(endpoint) {\n  try {\n    const response = await fetch(&#96;https://api.cursor.com${endpoint}&#96;, {\n      headers: {\n        'Authorization': &#96;Basic ${btoa(API_KEY + ':')}&#96;\n      }\n    });\n    \n    if (response.status === 429) {\n      // Rate limited - implement backoff\n      throw new Error('Rate limit exceeded');\n    }\n    \n    if (response.status === 401) {\n      // Invalid API key\n      throw new Error('Authentication failed');\n    }\n    \n    if (response.status === 403) {\n      // Insufficient permissions\n      throw new Error('Enterprise access required');\n    }\n    \n    if (!response.ok) {\n      throw new Error(&#96;API error: ${response.status}&#96;);\n    }\n    \n    return await response.json();\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error('API request failed:', error);\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Common Error Responses\n\nAll APIs use standard HTTP status codes:\n\n### 400 Bad Request\n\nRequest parameters are invalid or missing required fields.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"error\": \"Bad Request\",\n  \"message\": \"Some users are not in the team\"\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 401 Unauthorized\n\nInvalid or missing API key.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"error\": \"Unauthorized\",\n  \"message\": \"Invalid API key\"\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 403 Forbidden\n\nValid API key but insufficient permissions (e.g., Enterprise features on non-Enterprise plan).\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"error\": \"Forbidden\",\n  \"message\": \"Enterprise access required\"\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 404 Not Found\n\nRequested resource doesn't exist.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"error\": \"Not Found\",\n  \"message\": \"Resource not found\"\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 429 Too Many Requests\n\nRate limit exceeded. Implement exponential backoff.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"error\": \"Too Many Requests\",\n  \"message\": \"Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.\"\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 500 Internal Server Error\n\nServer-side error. Contact support if persistent.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"error\": \"Internal Server Error\",\n  \"message\": \"An unexpected error occurred\"\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Sitemap\n\n[Overview of all docs pages](https://cursor.com/llms.txt)"
}
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Checking cursor api**

Cursor has enterprise APIs for admin, analytics, and AI code tracking, but not for exporting chat transcripts. </details></think>

---


Cursor **有 API**，但主要是团队管理、用量分析和云 Agent 相关，**没有**用来导出本地聊天记录的接口。

---

## Cursor 官方 API 概览

| API | 用途 | 适用 |
|-----|------|------|
| **Admin API** | 成员、设置、用量、支出 | 企业版 |
| **Analytics API** | 用量、AI 指标、活跃用户 | 企业版 |
| **AI Code Tracking API** | 按提交/变更追踪 AI 生成代码 | 企业版 |
| **Cloud Agents API** | 创建、管理云端 AI 编码 Agent | Beta，各套餐 |

来源：[cursor.com/docs/api](https://cursor.com/docs/api)

---

## 和「导出聊天」的关系

- 这些 API 面向**团队管理和云端 Agent**，不涉及本地 Composer/Chat 的内容。
- 官方文档中**没有**提供：
  - 导出当前对话的 API  
  - 读取聊天历史的 API  
  - 程序化触发 Export Transcript 的 API  

所以：**Cursor 有 API，但都没有「导出聊天/对话」这一项**。  
目前只能通过：手动 Export Transcript，或 SpecStory 自动存到 `.specstory/history/`。

根据 Cursor 官方文档，可以这样处理：

---

## 1. 额度与套餐

| 套餐 | 月费 | 内含 API 额度 |
|------|------|--------------|
| Pro | $20/月 | $20 |
| **Pro Plus** | **$60/月** | **$70** |
| Ultra | $200/月 | $400 |

你说的「60 美元额度」大概率是 **Pro Plus 内含的 $70 额度** 用完了。

---

## 2. 可选做法

### 方式 A：开启按量付费（On-Demand）

- **入口**：[cursor.com/dashboard](https://cursor.com/dashboard) → 用量 / 设置
- **说明**：额度用完后按 API 价格继续计费（无加价）
- **建议**：在 Billing 里设一个**消费上限**，避免超支

### 方式 B：升级到更高套餐

- **Pro Plus → Ultra**：月费 $200，内含 $400 额度
- **升级入口**：Dashboard → Manage Subscription，或直接访问  
  `https://cursor.com/api/auth/checkoutDeepControl?tier=pro_plus`（升级到 Pro Plus）

### 方式 C：用自己的 API Key（推荐，长期更灵活）

- **路径**：Cursor Settings → Models → 找到 **Anthropic**
- **操作**：粘贴你的 **Anthropic API Key**
- **效果**：Claude 调用走你自己的额度，不再占用 Cursor 配额
- **注意**：Tab 补全仍用 Cursor 内置模型，不受影响

### 方式 D：等下月重置

- 每月在**订阅日**自动重置（如 15 号订阅，每月 15 号重置）
- 未用完的额度不会顺延到下月

---

## 3. 建议组合

- **短期**：先用 **方式 A（按量）** 或等 **方式 D（下月重置）**
- **长期**：申请 Anthropic 账号并充值，在 Cursor 里填 **方式 C（自己的 API Key）**，既省 Cursor 配额，又能按自己用量控制成本

---

_**User**_

我现在额度满了是不是等待一段时间就恢复了？

---

_**Agent (model composer-1.5, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Checking cursor limits**

I confirmed that Cursor usage resets monthly on the subscription date, not hourly or daily. </details></think>

---


是的，会恢复，但**是按月重置**，不是隔几小时或几天。

---

## 重置规则（官方）

| 说明 | 内容 |
|------|------|
| **重置时间** | 每月在**你的订阅日**重置 |
| **举例** | 若 3 月 15 日订阅，则每月 15 日左右重置 |
| **未用完额度** | 不会顺延到下个月 |

---

## 怎么看自己的重置日期？

打开 [cursor.com/dashboard](https://cursor.com/dashboard) → **Manage Subscription**，会看到下一次计费 / 重置日期。

---

**结论**：要等额度恢复，只能等到下个计费周期。在此之前，可以选择：开启按量付费，或使用自己的 Anthropic API Key。

升级到 Ultra（$200/月）后，可以在本地 Cursor 里这样查看用量：

---

## 1. 应用内用量概览（本地）

在 Cursor 里打开设置：

- **Windows/Linux**：`Ctrl + Shift + J`
- **macOS**：`Cmd + Shift + J`

或点击右上角齿轮 → 设置。

在设置搜索框输入 **`usage`** 或 **`usage summary`**，找到：

- **"When to show the usage summary"**（何时显示用量摘要）
- 设为 **"Always"**（始终）

之后在 Chat 输入框下方会显示类似：

> 你已使用本月额度约 XX%

---

## 2. 查看更详细的用量（网页）

在浏览器中打开：

- [cursor.com/dashboard](https://cursor.com/dashboard) → Usage
- 或 [cursor.com/settings](https://cursor.com/settings) → Usage

可以看到：

- 本月已用/剩余额度
- 按模型分的 token 用量
- 按量计费明细（如有）

---

## 3. 可选：用量扩展

在扩展市场（`Ctrl + Shift + X`）搜索 **"Cursor Usage Monitor"** 或 **"lixen.cursor-usage"**，安装后可在状态栏显示用量（部分版本可能需要配置 cookie）。

---

**总结**：本地主要靠设置里的 **Usage Summary = Always** 看大致用量；精确数据要去网页 Dashboard 的 Usage 页面。

---


是的，就是在这个位置显示的。

---

## 1. 显示位置

截图里 **"You've used 23% of your included total usage"** 就是用量摘要，在 Chat 输入框下方、模型选择（∞ Agent / Composer 1.5）下面。

---

## 2. 这个数字的含义

**23%** 表示：你已经用掉了当月**内含额度**的 23%。

- **included total usage** = 你订阅套餐里的月 API 额度（如 Pro 的 $20、Pro Plus 的 $70、Ultra 的 $400）
- 你在 Agent / Composer 里用的 Claude、GPT 等，都会一起算进这个总额度
- 用多少，这个百分比就会上涨；到 100% 就是当月额度用尽

---

## 3. 是否指“Claude 模型”？

**不单指 Claude**，而是**所有计入额度的模型总和**。

你现在用的是 **Composer 1.5**（背后可能是 Claude），但这里统计的是你在 Cursor 里用过的所有付费模型，例如：

- Composer / Claude
- GPT 等其它模型

只要用了这些模型，都会算进这 23%。所以它代表的是**整体 API 用量**，而不是只有 Claude。

---


整理了一份适合你（用国内 Visa 或人民币）买 AI API 的方案：

---

## 1. 虚拟卡（在官网自绑卡时用）

**国内 Visa 无法直接在 OpenAI / Anthropic 官网支付**（Stripe 会拒）。需要先用人民币充值虚拟卡，再用虚拟卡绑官网。

| 平台 | 充值方式 | 适用场景 |
|------|----------|----------|
| **HUTAO（胡桃）** | 支付宝 | 2026 常用，支持 OpenAI、Claude 等 |
| **Fomepay** | 支付宝/微信 | 虚拟 Visa/Mastercard |
| **BinGoCard** | 支付宝 | 虚拟卡 |
| **Coinepay** | USDT (TRC20) | 数字货币充值 |

说明：虚拟卡发卡地在美国等境外，因此能被 Stripe 接受；你这边用支付宝/微信给虚拟卡充人民币即可。

---

## 2. 聚合/中转平台（直接用人民币，不必绑卡）

用人民币在平台上买 API 额度，平台给你 Key 或代理接口，一般**支持支付宝 / 微信 / 网银**，是否支持国内 Visa 需在各平台页面确认。

| 平台 | 特点 |
|------|------|
| **147API** | 约官方 5 折，支持支付宝、对公 |
| **AIHub 智慧代理** | 多模型聚合，价格较低 |
| **DeepFlow 深流** | 低汇率，多模型 |
| **简易 API** | 价格较低，约 2 元 ≈ 1 美元额度 |

这些平台多数是人民币结算，用不上「刷中国 Visa」，但方便，无绑卡风控。

---

## 3. 推荐路线

| 需求 | 建议 |
|------|------|
| **想直接给 Cursor 填 Anthropic Key** | 用 **HUTAO** 充值 → 去 anthropic.com 开通并绑卡 → 拿到 API Key |
| **不想绑卡，只想买额度** | 用 **147API / AIHub** 等中转平台，支付宝/微信充值 |
| **坚持用中国 Visa 刷卡** | 先查各聚合平台是否支持 Visa 支付（官网或客服），若有则可在平台内刷卡充值 |

---

## 4. 使用注意

- 用虚拟卡绑官网：需配合稳定美国 IP，账单地址填美国地址
- 用聚合平台：拿到的是平台 Key，在 Cursor 里把「Models」改成自定义 Anthropic/OpenAI 的 Base URL 和 Key
- 避免拼车、共享账号，风险高

可以按「是否愿意绑境外虚拟卡」选方案：愿意绑卡 → HUTAO；不愿意 → 聚合平台。

[Override openai base url - Discussions - Cursor - Community Forum](https://forum.cursor.com/t/override-openai-base-url/152006)<br/><br/># Cursor Custom OpenAI API Endpoint Proxy (2026)<br/><br/>## Available Solutions<br/><br/>**cursor-api-proxy** is a TypeScript project that creates an OpenAI-compatible proxy for Cursor CLI.[1] It exposes Cursor models on localhost so any LLM client (OpenAI SDK, LiteLLM, LangChain) can call them as a standard chat API.[1]<br/><br/>### Key Features:[1]<br/>- Runs on `http://127.0.0.1:8765` by default<br/>- Supports streaming for both OpenAI and Anthropic APIs<br/>- Can be exposed via Tailscale with HTTPS/MagicDNS<br/>- Requires Cursor CLI (`agent`) version 2026.02.27 or later and Node.js 18+<br/>- Supports optional API key authentication via `CURSOR_BRIDGE_API_KEY`<br/><br/>### Endpoints provided:[1]<br/>- `/v1/models` - List available Cursor models<br/>- `/v1/chat/completions` - OpenAI-compatible chat API<br/>- `/v1/messages` - Anthropic Messages API<br/><br/>## Native Cursor Support<br/><br/>Cursor IDE itself supports custom OpenAI API keys and custom endpoints directly in Settings > Models.[2] However, the Cursor CLI's `--endpoint` and `--api-key` parameters have had limited success with personal API keys according to community feedback.[4]<br/><br/>## Use Cases<br/><br/>This proxy enables use of alternative LLM gateways and self-hosted models while maintaining compatibility with Cursor's agent runtime.[4][5] |

[GitHub - easychen/openai-api-proxy: 一行Docker命令部署的 OpenAI/GPT API代理，支持SSE流式返回、腾讯云函数 。Simple proxy for OpenAi api via a one-line docker command](https://github.com/easychen/openai-api-proxy)<br/><br/># OpenAI 兼容 API 接口格式和 Base URL 总结<br/><br/>## Base URL 格式<br/><br/>OpenAI 兼容接口的标准 base URL 结构为：<br/><br/>```<br/>https://api.example.com/v1/<br/>```<br/><br/>常见的 OpenAI 兼容 API 服务的 base URL 示例：[1][2]<br/><br/>- **OpenAI-Hub**: `https://api.openai-hub.com` （原官方为 `https://api.openai.com`，只需在末尾加 `-hub`）[1]<br/>- **智增增 API**: `https://api.zhizengzeng.com/v1/` [2]<br/><br/>## 核心接口端点<br/><br/>标准 OpenAI 兼容格式包含：[3][4]<br/><br/>```<br/>GET  /v1/models                    # 获取模型列表<br/>POST /v1/chat/completions          # 聊天完成接口<br/>GET  /health                       # 健康检查<br/>```<br/><br/>## 请求格式<br/><br/>### HTTP 头部要求 [4]<br/><br/>```<br/>Content-Type: application/json<br/>Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY<br/>Accept: application/json<br/>```<br/><br/>### 聊天完成请求示例 (POST /v1/chat/completions) [2][4]<br/><br/>```json<br/>\{<br/>  "model": "gpt-3.5-turbo",<br/>  "messages": [<br/>    \{"role": "system", "content": "系统提示"\},<br/>    \{"role": "user", "content": "用户消息"\}<br/>  ],<br/>  "temperature": 0.7,<br/>  "max_tokens": 1000,<br/>  "stream": false<br/>\}<br/>```<br/><br/>## 响应格式 [4]<br/><br/>非流式响应包含：<br/>- `id`: 响应唯一标识符<br/>- `object`: "chat.completion"<br/>- `created`: 时间戳<br/>- `model`: 使用的模型名<br/>- `choices`: 生成的选择列表<br/>- `usage`: Token 使用统计<br/><br/>流式响应使用 Server-Sent Events (SSE) 格式，以 `data:` 开头。[4]<br/><br/>## 使用示例 [2]<br/><br/>在 Python 中配置客户端：<br/><br/>```python<br/>from openai import OpenAI<br/><br/>client = OpenAI(<br/>    api_key="your-api-key",<br/>    base_url="https://api.zhizengzeng.com/v1/"  # 替换官方域名<br/>)<br/>```<br/><br/>只需修改 `base_url` 参数即可指向兼容的 OpenAI API 服务器。[2][3] |

**支持。** 只要简易 API 提供的是 **OpenAI 兼容** 的接口，就可以在 Cursor 里用。

---

## Cursor 的配置方式

Cursor 支持自定义 API，在 **Settings → Models** 里可以：

1. 选择 **Add Model** / 添加自定义模型  
2. 填写：
   - **API Type**：选 **OpenAI Compatible**
   - **API URL**：简易 API 提供的 Base URL（如 `https://api.xxx.com/v1`）
   - **API Key**：简易 API 的密钥

---

## 前提条件

简易 API 需要符合：

- 接口格式与 OpenAI 一致（`/v1/chat/completions` 等）
- 提供 Base URL 和 API Key
- 模型名与 Cursor 能识别的兼容（如 `gpt-4`、`gpt-3.5-turbo`）

大部分国内聚合 / 中转平台都做成 OpenAI 兼容，一般可以直接用。

---

## 配置示例

若简易 API 提供：

- Base URL：`https://api.jianyi-xxx.com/v1`
- API Key：`sk-xxxx`

在 Cursor 里：

1. 打开 **Settings**（`Ctrl + Shift + J`）
2. 进入 **Models** 或 **Custom Models**
3. 添加模型，填入上述 Base URL 和 API Key，类型选 **OpenAI Compatible**
4. 用 **Test Connection** 测试是否可用

配置成功后，即可在 Cursor 里用简易 API 的模型，消耗的是简易 API 的额度，而不是 Cursor 自带的额度。

_**Agent (model composer-1.5, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Configuring API tokens**

I'm analyzing the API token configuration interface to understand how to properly set up group priority selections. </details></think>

---


从你给的截图来看，这是 API 平台的令牌配置界面，可以这样理解：

---

## 1. 分组优先级选择

**含义**：该令牌会按顺序使用不同的**转发分组**（不同线路/节点）。

- **选 2～3 个分组**：主用第一个，失败再试第二、第三个
- **选择顺序 = 优先级**：先选的为主分组，后面是备用

**建议**：  
选 2～3 个分组，按你更信任/更快的顺序排列，保证稳定性。

---

## 2. 过期时间

**含义**：令牌什么时候失效。

| 选项 | 含义 | 适用 |
|------|------|------|
| 永不过期 | 长期有效 | 个人长期用、绑定 Cursor 时 |
| 一个月 | 约 30 天后失效 | 定期更换、控制风险 |
| 一天 / 一小时 | 短期有效 | 临时测试 |

**建议**：  
长期用选 **永不过期**；有安全顾虑选 **一个月**，到期再新建令牌。

---

## 3. 额度设置 + 无限额度

**含义**：
- **无限额度 = 开**：不按额度限制，但可能按平台账户余额扣费
- **无限额度 = 关**：只按令牌的「额度」扣，用完就不能再调 API

**建议**：  
自己用且想省心，可开 **无限额度**；想控制成本，关掉后设置具体额度。

---

## 4. 自动选择里的倍数（2.4倍、0.1倍、0.0倍）

**含义**：不同模型对额度/余额的消耗系数。

| 模型 | 倍数 | 含义 |
|------|------|------|
| **Claude Code专属** | 2.4倍 | 调用一次 ≈ 基准消耗 × 2.4，较贵 |
| **Codex专属** | 0.1倍 | 消耗约为基准的 0.1 倍，较便宜 |
| **MJ悦速** | 0.0倍 | 一般表示不计费或极低 |

**建议**：  
如果用 Cursor + Claude，主要会走 **Claude Code 专属**，消耗会按 2.4 倍计，可以多充一些额度或打开无限额度。

---

## 推荐配置（给 Cursor 用）

| 项目 | 建议 |
|------|------|
| 分组 | 选 2～3 个分组，按稳定/延迟排序 |
| 过期时间 | **永不过期** |
| 额度 | 开 **无限额度**，或设一个较大的额度 |
| 自动选择 | 若只用 Claude，可只勾选 **Claude Code 专属**；若也会用 Codex，可一起勾上 |

保存后，把该平台提供的 **API Base URL** 和这个 **令牌** 填到 Cursor 的 Models 设置里使用即可。

**Configuring model selection**

I need to clarify that users must configure the specific model name in Cursor's custom model settings when using the API aggregation platform. </details></think>

---


**要配置。** 模型型号有两个地方会用到：

---

## 1. 在 Cursor 里配置（必配）

在 **Settings → Models → Add Model** 或编辑已有自定义模型时，需要填：

| 字段 | 作用 | 示例 |
|------|------|------|
| **Model ID** | 告诉 API 用哪个模型 | `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`、`gpt-4o` |
| **API URL** | 简易 API 的 Base URL | `https://api.xxx.com/v1` |
| **API Key** | 简易 API 给你的令牌 | `sk-xxxx` |

**Model ID** 必须和简易 API 支持的型号**完全一致**，否则会报错或走错模型。

---

## 2. 在简易 API 平台里的配置（视平台而定）

有的平台会有：

- **分组 / 通道**：不同分组对应不同模型或供应商
- **模型权限**：你的令牌能调用哪些模型

如果之前选的分组里有 Claude，一般就会开放对应的 Claude 模型；具体要查平台文档或后台说明。

---

## 3. 如何知道该填什么 Model ID？

需要看简易 API 官方文档或后台的「模型列表」，常见格式类似：

| 平台类型 | Claude 示例 |
|----------|-------------|
| OpenAI 兼容接口 | `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`、`claude-3-opus` 等 |
| Anthropic 原生接口 | 可能用 `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022` |

可以：

1. 在简易 API 后台找「模型列表」「API 文档」「支持的模型」
2. 或在他们的帮助/客服里问：**“Claude 3.5 Sonnet 的 model ID 是什么？”**

---

## 4. 在 Cursor 中的操作步骤

1. 打开 **Settings**（`Ctrl + Shift + J`）→ **Models**
2. 点击 **Add Model**（或编辑已有自定义模型）
3. 填写：
   - **API Type**：`OpenAI Compatible` 或 `Anthropic`（按平台说明选）
   - **API URL**：简易 API 的 Base URL
   - **API Key**：你的令牌
   - **Model ID**：从简易 API 文档里拿到的具体型号

保存后，在 Cursor 的模型下拉里就会出现这个模型，选它就会走简易 API 的额度。

**总结**：模型型号在 Cursor 的 Model ID 里配置，必须和简易 API 支持的型号一致，具体写什么要去平台文档查。

---


**不是。** 这是管理后台地址，不是 API 的 Base URL。

---

## 区别说明

| 地址类型 | 示例 | 用途 |
|----------|------|------|
| **管理后台** | `jeniya.cn/console/token` | 在网页上管理令牌、看用量 |
| **API Base URL** | 如 `https://api.jeniya.cn/v1` | 真正用来发 API 请求的地址 |

你截的 `jeniya.cn/console/token` 属于第一种，不能填进 Cursor 的 API URL。

---

## 如何找到正确的 Base URL

1. 在左侧菜单点击 **「API文档」**
2. 在文档里查找：
   - 「接口地址」
   - 「Base URL」
   - 「API 端点」
   - 或类似「请求示例」中的域名

通常格式类似：

- `https://api.jeniya.cn/v1`
- `https://jeniya.cn/api/v1`
- `https://openai.jeniya.cn/v1`

具体以文档为准，找到后再填到 Cursor 的 **API URL** 里。

随着 Claude 4.0 系列模型在 2025 年末及 2026 年初的全面普及，其卓越的逻辑推理、长文本处理以及近乎人类的编程能力，已使其成为全球开发者、科研人员及企业的首选 AI 引擎。

然而，国内开发者在接入 Anthropic 官方 API 时，依然面临着“三座大山”：

支付门槛高：必须绑定海外信用卡，拒卡率极高。💳
网络环境不稳定：直连官方接口经常出现超时或断连。🌐
账号风控严：动辄封号，导致业务中断。🚫
为了解决这些痛点，Claude API 中转站应运而生。本文将基于 2026 年最新的市场数据，深度评测国内主流中转平台，并分享如何通过 jeniya.cn 等优质渠道实现低成本、高稳定的 AI 接入。你研究一下

📊 2026 年 Claude 模型定价与中转优势
进入 2026 年，Claude 系列模型已经演进至 4.0 时代。以下是官方定价与优质中转服务的对比：

模型版本	官方价格 (输入/输出 每M Tokens)	中转站价格 (约 2-5 折)	核心适用场景
Claude 4.0 Opus	$15 / $75	$4.5 / $22.5	复杂架构设计、深度学术论文、高难度编程
Claude 4.0 Sonnet	$3 / $15	$0.9 / $4.5	通用对话、日常代码辅助、中等复杂度任务
Claude 3.5 Haiku	$0.25 / $1.25	$0.05 / $0.25	极速响应、自动化脚本、大规模文本分类
💡 为什么选择中转站？
节省成本：通过聚合采购，中转站通常能提供官方 2-8 折的价格。
本地支付：直接支持支付宝、微信支付，告别海外卡烦恼。💰
极速响应：中转站通常在香港、新加坡等地部署了中转节点，大幅降低国内访问延迟。
简单易用：100% 兼容 OpenAI 格式或 Anthropic 原生格式，换个 Base_URL 即可无缝迁移。
🛠️ 技术架构深度解析：中转站是如何工作的？
一个优秀的 API 中转站（如 jeniya.cn）不仅仅是简单的请求转发，其背后有一套复杂的优化机制：

智能路由层：实时监控各地上游接口的健康状况，自动剔除故障节点。
高并发负载均衡：通过分布式集群处理海量并发请求，确保在高负载下依然维持 <200ms 的首字响应速度。⚡
协议转换：支持将 Claude 接口转换为 OpenAI 兼容格式，让原本只支持 GPT 的插件和软件（如 NextChat, OneAPI）秒变 Claude。
安全合规：采用 TLS 1.3 加密，承诺不审计、不存储用户的对话私密内容。🔒
🧪 2026 实测数据：性能表现
我们对 jeniya.cn 提供的 Claude API 进行了为期 30 天的压力测试，结果如下：

**可用性 (Uptime)：99.92%**。在测试期间，几乎未遇到服务中断。
**平均延迟 (Latency)**：
Claude 4.0 Sonnet：120ms (首字响应)
Claude 4.0 Opus：280ms (首字响应)
并发极限：支持最高 2000 RPM（每分钟请求数），完全满足企业级生产环境需求。
💻 快速上手指南：3分钟接入 Claude API
通过 jeniya.cn 接入 Claude 非常简单，以下是 Python 示例：

import anthropic

# 1. 替换为您在中转站获取的 API Key
# 2. 修改 base_url 为中转站提供的地址
client = anthropic.Anthropic(
    api_key="sk-xxxxyour_key_here",
    base_url="https://api.jeniya.cn/v1" 
)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "请分析2026年AI大模型的发展趋势。"}
    ]
)

print(message.content)
小贴士：如果您使用的是 OpenAI SDK 或相关的开源项目，只需将 BASE_URL 设置为 https://api.jeniya.cn/v1 即可。

Q: 支持 Claude 4.0 最新的 Vision（图像识别）功能吗？ A: 完美支持！jeniya.cn 保持与官方同步更新，无论是多模态视觉识别，还是最新的 Artifacts 功能，均可正常使用。🖼️

Q: 计费方式是怎样的？ A: 采用按量计费（Pay-as-you-go），充值多少用多少，额度永久有效，非常适合开发者调试或小规模团队使用。

title: Auto Router
subtitle: Automatically select the best model for your prompt
headline: Auto Router | Smart AI Model Selection
canonical-url: '[https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/routing/routers/auto-router](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/routing/routers/auto-router)'
'og:site\_name': OpenRouter Documentation
'og:title': Auto Router - Intelligent Model Selection
'og:description': >-
Automatically select the best AI model for your prompts using OpenRouter's
Auto Router powered by NotDiamond.
'og:image':
type: url
value: >-
[https://openrouter.ai/dynamic-og?title=Auto%20Router\&description=Intelligent%20AI%20model%20selection](https://openrouter.ai/dynamic-og?title=Auto%20Router\&description=Intelligent%20AI%20model%20selection)
'og:image:width': 1200

'twitter:card': summary\_large\_image
'twitter:site': '@OpenRouter'
-----------------------------

The [Auto Router](https://openrouter.ai/openrouter/auto) (`openrouter/auto`) automatically selects the best model for your prompt, powered by [NotDiamond](https://www.notdiamond.ai/).

## Overview

Instead of manually choosing a model, let the Auto Router analyze your prompt and select the optimal model from a curated set of high-quality options. The router considers factors like prompt complexity, task type, and model capabilities.

## Usage

Set your model to `openrouter/auto`:

<CodeGroup>
  ```typescript title="TypeScript SDK"
  import { OpenRouter } from '@openrouter/sdk';

  const openRouter = new OpenRouter({
    apiKey: '<OPENROUTER_API_KEY>',
  });

  const completion = await openRouter.chat.send({
    model: 'openrouter/auto',
    messages: [
      {
        role: 'user',
        content: 'Explain quantum entanglement in simple terms',
      },
    ],
  });

  console.log(completion.choices[0].message.content);
  // Check which model was selected
  console.log('Model used:', completion.model);
  ```

  ```typescript title="TypeScript (fetch)"
  const response = await fetch('https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': 'Bearer <OPENROUTER_API_KEY>',
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      model: 'openrouter/auto',
      messages: [
        {
          role: 'user',
          content: 'Explain quantum entanglement in simple terms',
        },
      ],
    }),
  });

  const data = await response.json();
  console.log(data.choices[0].message.content);
  // Check which model was selected
  console.log('Model used:', data.model);
  ```

  ```python title="Python"
  import requests
  import json

  response = requests.post(
    url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions",
    headers={
      "Authorization": "Bearer <OPENROUTER_API_KEY>",
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
    },
    data=json.dumps({
      "model": "openrouter/auto",
      "messages": [
        {
          "role": "user",
          "content": "Explain quantum entanglement in simple terms"
        }
      ]
    })
  )

  data = response.json()
  print(data['choices'][0]['message']['content'])
  # Check which model was selected
  print('Model used:', data['model'])
  ```
</CodeGroup>

## Response

The response includes the `model` field showing which model was actually used:

```json
{
  "id": "gen-...",
  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5",  // The model that was selected
  "choices": [
    {
      "message": {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": "..."
      }
    }
  ],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 15,
    "completion_tokens": 150,
    "total_tokens": 165
  }
}
```

## How It Works

1. **Prompt Analysis**: Your prompt is analyzed by NotDiamond's routing system
2. **Model Selection**: The optimal model is selected based on the task requirements
3. **Request Forwarding**: Your request is forwarded to the selected model
4. **Response Tracking**: The response includes metadata showing which model was used

## Supported Models

The Auto Router selects from a curated set of high-quality models including:

<Callout intent="warning">
  Model slugs change as new versions are released. The examples below are current as of December 4, 2025. Check the [models page](https://openrouter.ai/models) for the latest available models.
</Callout>

* Claude Sonnet 4.5 (`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`)
* Claude Opus 4.5 (`anthropic/claude-opus-4.5`)
* GPT-5.1 (`openai/gpt-5.1`)
* Gemini 3 Pro (`google/gemini-3-pro-preview`)
* DeepSeek 3.2 (`deepseek/deepseek-v3.2`)
* And other top-performing models

The exact model pool may be updated as new models become available.

## Configuring Allowed Models

You can restrict which models the Auto Router can select from using the `plugins` parameter. This is useful when you want to limit routing to specific providers or model families.

### Via API Request

Use wildcard patterns to filter models. For example, `anthropic/*` matches all Anthropic models:

<CodeGroup>
  ```typescript title="TypeScript SDK"
  const completion = await openRouter.chat.send({
    model: 'openrouter/auto',
    messages: [
      {
        role: 'user',
        content: 'Explain quantum entanglement',
      },
    ],
    plugins: [
      {
        id: 'auto-router',
        allowed_models: ['anthropic/*', 'openai/gpt-5.1'],
      },
    ],
  });
  ```

  ```typescript title="TypeScript (fetch)"
  const response = await fetch('https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      Authorization: 'Bearer <OPENROUTER_API_KEY>',
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      model: 'openrouter/auto',
      messages: [
        {
          role: 'user',
          content: 'Explain quantum entanglement',
        },
      ],
      plugins: [
        {
          id: 'auto-router',
          allowed_models: ['anthropic/*', 'openai/gpt-5.1'],
        },
      ],
    }),
  });
  ```

  ```python title="Python"
  response = requests.post(
    url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions",
    headers={
      "Authorization": "Bearer <OPENROUTER_API_KEY>",
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
    },
    data=json.dumps({
      "model": "openrouter/auto",
      "messages": [
        {
          "role": "user",
          "content": "Explain quantum entanglement"
        }
      ],
      "plugins": [
        {
          "id": "auto-router",
          "allowed_models": ["anthropic/*", "openai/gpt-5.1"]
        }
      ]
    })
  )
  ```
</CodeGroup>

### Via Settings UI

You can also configure default allowed models in your [Plugin Settings](https://openrouter.ai/settings/plugins):

1. Navigate to **Settings > Plugins**
2. Find **Auto Router** and click the configure button
3. Enter model patterns (one per line)
4. Save your settings

These defaults apply to all your API requests unless overridden per-request.

### Pattern Syntax

| Pattern          | Matches                                |
| ---------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `anthropic/*`    | All Anthropic models                   |
| `openai/gpt-5*`  | All GPT-5 variants                     |
| `google/*`       | All Google models                      |
| `openai/gpt-5.1` | Exact match only                       |
| `*/claude-*`     | Any provider with claude in model name |

When no patterns are configured, the Auto Router uses all supported models.

## Pricing

You pay the standard rate for whichever model is selected. There is no additional fee for using the Auto Router.

## Use Cases

* **General-purpose applications**: When you don't know what types of prompts users will send
* **Cost optimization**: Let the router choose efficient models for simpler tasks
* **Quality optimization**: Ensure complex prompts get routed to capable models
* **Experimentation**: Discover which models work best for your use case

## Limitations

* The router requires `messages` format (not `prompt`)
* Streaming is supported
* All standard OpenRouter features (tool calling, etc.) work with the selected model

## Related

* [Body Builder](/docs/guides/routing/routers/body-builder) - Generate multiple parallel API requests
* [Model Fallbacks](/docs/guides/routing/model-fallbacks) - Configure fallback models
* [Provider Selection](/docs/guides/routing/provider-selection) - Control which providers are used

---


## OpenRouter Auto Router 简要分析

---

## 1. 功能概述

**Auto Router** 会根据你的 prompt **自动选择合适模型**，不必每次手动选模型。

| 项目 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| **Model ID** | `openrouter/auto` |
| **底层** | NotDiamond 的路由系统 |
| **原理** | 分析 prompt → 按任务复杂度、类型选择模型 → 转发到对应模型 |

---

## 2. 工作流程

```
你的 prompt → NotDiamond 分析 → 选中一个模型 → 转发请求 → 返回结果
```

返回里会带上 `model` 字段，说明实际使用的模型。

---

## 3. 支持的模型池（示例）

文档中提到的候选包括（以官方最新为准）：

- Claude Sonnet 4.5
- Claude Opus 4.5
- GPT-5.1
- Gemini 3 Pro
- DeepSeek 3.2
- 其他当前 top 模型

---

## 4. 与 Cursor 配合的用法

在 Cursor 中只配置 **一个模型**：`openrouter/auto`，即可自动路由到合适模型。

| 配置项 | 填写 |
|--------|------|
| **API URL** | `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1` |
| **API Key** | OpenRouter 密钥 |
| **Model ID** | `openrouter/auto` |

这样 Cursor 会把所有请求发到 Auto Router，由它自动选模型。

---

## 5. 可选：限制使用的模型

通过 `plugins` 的 `allowed_models` 可以只允许特定模型：

| 模式 | 含义 |
|------|------|
| `anthropic/*` | 只用 Anthropic（Claude） |
| `openai/gpt-5*` | 只用 GPT-5 系列 |
| `anthropic/*` + `openai/gpt-5.1` | Claude 或 GPT-5.1 |

配置方式：

1. 在 API 请求里加 `plugins` 参数；
2. 或在 OpenRouter **Settings → Plugins → Auto Router** 里设默认规则。

**注意**：Cursor 的自定义模型配置通常不能传 `plugins`，所以默认会使用 Auto Router 的全部候选模型；如需限制，要靠 OpenRouter 后台默认配置。

---

## 6. 计费

- 只按实际被选中模型的价格收费；
- 没有额外 Auto Router 费用。

---

## 7. 优点与局限

| 优点 | 局限 |
|------|------|
| 不用手动选模型，减少试错 | Cursor 可能无法传 `plugins` 做限制 |
| 简单任务用便宜模型，复杂任务用强模型 | 路由逻辑不透明，可能选不到你预期的模型 |
| 一个 model ID 覆盖多种任务 | 需要 `messages` 格式，不支持老的 `prompt` |
| 支持流式、tool calling | - |

---

## 8. 使用建议

| 场景 | 建议 |
|------|------|
| 想省心、不想挑模型 | 用 `openrouter/auto` |
| 只用 Claude，不想用其他 | 在 OpenRouter 后台配置 `allowed_models: anthropic/*` |
| 要精确控制成本 | 直接选具体模型（如 `anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet`） |
| 代码 / 编程为主 | Auto Router 一般会偏好 Claude、GPT-5 等适合编码的模型 |

**结论**：把 Cursor 的 Model ID 设成 `openrouter/auto`，就能自动选模型，适合不想折腾模型选择、接受按需切换的情况。

---

结论先说：**多半会记录**，但要实际验证一次才确定。

---

## 1. SpecStory 里本来就有模型信息

从 `export_transcript.py` 的清洗逻辑可以看出，SpecStory 存档里有这样的格式：

```
_**Agent (model xxx, mode xxx)**_
```

说明每条回复都会带上 **model** 和 **mode** 两个字段。

---

## 2. Auto 模式下具体会记成什么？

两种可能：

| 情况 | 记录内容 | 说明 |
|------|----------|------|
| **A** | 每次实际用到的模型 | 例如：Composer 1.5、GPT-5.3 Codex 等 |
| **B** | 只记成「Auto」 | 只记录模式，不记录具体模型 |

取决于 Cursor 在把数据给 SpecStory 时，是否把 Auto 解析后的**实际模型名**一并传下去。

---

## 3. 怎么自己验证？

1. 打开一个新的 Composer 窗口，选 **Auto Efficiency**
2. 发几条不同类型的请求（简单 / 复杂各几条）
3. 等 SpecStory 自动保存（或手动 Save）
4. 打开 `.specstory/history/` 里对应文件，搜索：
   - `_**Agent (model` 或 `model `
5. 看这里写的是：
   - `model Composer 1.5`、`model GPT-5.3 Codex` 等具体名称 → 说明会记录实际模型
   - `model Auto` 或只有模式 → 说明只记录了「Auto」，没有具体模型

---

## 4. 如果想 100% 拿到「实际用到的模型」

- **OpenRouter `openrouter/auto`**：API 返回里有 `model` 字段，明确写出本次实际调用的模型。
- **Cursor 内置 Auto**：依赖 Cursor 和 SpecStory 的实现，不一定暴露具体模型，需要按上面步骤实测。

**建议**：先用 Cursor Auto 跑几条对话，然后打开对应 SpecStory 存档看一眼 `model` 内容，就能确认会不会记录当时使用的模型。

2. **2026-03-13_06-59Z-新窗口任务和完成情况.md** - shows `model default` - this might be when they had "default" or Auto selected; "default" doesn't tell us the actual model used

So the format is clear: SpecStory captures `_**Agent (model XXX, mode YYY)**_`. When a specific model is chosen, we see the full model name (e.g. claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking). When it's "default" we don't know if that's Auto or the default Cursor model - and "default" doesn't reveal which concrete model was used.

For a proper test of Auto mode, the user would need to:
1. Start a new chat
2. Select "Auto Efficiency" 
3. Send a few messages
4. Save with SpecStory (or wait for auto-save)
5. Open the new file and check - do we see a concrete model name (e.g. Composer 1.5, GPT-5.3) or do we see "default" or "Auto"?

Let me also check if there are any files with "Auto" or other model names.

   - `composer-1.5`

2. **When it's "default"** → SpecStory records `model default` - this doesn't tell us the concrete model (could be Auto's resolution, or Cursor's default). So when using "Auto" or default, we get "default" in the file, NOT the actual model that was used for each turn.

So the test result is:

- **Auto / default**: ❌ 只记 "default"，不会记录当时实际用的模型

已经用你现有的 SpecStory 存档跑了一遍，结论如下。

---

## 测试结果（基于现有存档）

| 你选的模式 | SpecStory 里记录的 model |
|------------|---------------------------|
| **指定具体模型** | ✅ **会记具体型号** |
| **Auto / 默认** | ❌ **只记 `default`** |

### 具体例子

**会记录具体模型的文件：**

- `回家测试系统.md` → `model claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking`
- `n8n-data-cleaning-for-specstory.md` → `claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking` / `claude-4.6-sonnet-medium-thinking`
- `文件搜索和关键词匹配.md` → `model composer-1.5`
- `n8n-workflow-configuration.md` → `model claude-4.5-sonnet`

**只记成 default 的文件（多半是 Auto 或默认模式）：**

- `新窗口任务和完成情况.md` → `model default`
- `ai回复与流程简化.md` → `model default`

---

## 结论

- **手动选具体模型时**：SpecStory 会记录当时使用的模型（例如 `claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking`、`composer-1.5`）。
- **用 Cursor 的 Auto（自动）模式时**：SpecStory 里只会看到 `model default`，**不会**记录当时实际用的是 Composer 1.5 还是 GPT-5.3 等。

所以：**用 Auto 时，SpecStory 不会记录「当时用的具体模型」**，只会是 `default`。若需要每条回复对应到具体模型，要么手动选模型，要么走 OpenRouter 的 `openrouter/auto`（API 返回里会带实际 `model`）。

## 已完成的配置

1. **沙盒放行**：已在 `.cursor/sandbox.json` 中加入 `openrouter.ai` 和 `api.openrouter.ai`
2. **配置指南**：已创建 `数字分身/AI产出/系统搭建/[产出] OpenRouter-Auto配置指南-2026-03-14.md`

---

## 你需要做的（3 步）

### 步骤 1：Cursor 中添加模型

1. 打开 Cursor → **Settings**（`Ctrl + Shift + J`）→ **Models**
2. 点击 **Add Model**
3. 填写：
   - **API Type**：`OpenAI Compatible`
   - **API URL**：`https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`
   - **API Key**：从 https://openrouter.ai/keys 复制
   - **Model ID**：`openrouter/auto`

### 步骤 2：在 OpenRouter 限制为 3 个模型

1. 打开 https://openrouter.ai/settings/plugins
2. 找到 **Auto Router** → 打开配置
3. 在 **Allowed Models** 中，每行一个填写：

```
anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6
anthropic/claude-opus-4.6
anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5
```

如需 Opus 4.5，再加一行：`anthropic/claude-opus-4.5`

### 步骤 3：充值与验证

1. 在 https://openrouter.ai/credits 用支付宝充值
2. 在 Cursor 模型列表中选中刚添加的 OpenRouter 模型
3. 发一条消息测试

---

## 效果

Auto Router 会根据任务复杂度，在 **Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.5** 之间自动切换，SpecStory 会记录每次实际使用的模型。

详细说明见：`数字分身/AI产出/系统搭建/[产出] OpenRouter-Auto配置指南-2026-03-14.md`。

已经查清并更新了配置，结论如下：

---

## 1. Step 3.5 Flash 是什么？

| 项目 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| **提供商** | StepFun（阶趣） |
| **OpenRouter Model ID** | `stepfun/step-3.5-flash` |
| **免费版** | `stepfun/step-3.5-flash:free` |
| **架构** | 196B MoE，每次约激活 11B 参数 |
| **定价** | $0.1/M 入、$0.3/M 出（远低于 Claude） |
| **特点** | 速度快、成本低，综合性价比高 |

排名靠前是因为速度快、价格低，适合日常和高频调用。

---

## 2. OpenRouter 的 Allowed Models 建议

在 OpenRouter 后台配置 Allowed Models 时，可以写成：

```
anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6
anthropic/claude-opus-4.6
stepfun/step-3.5-flash
anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5
```

配置指南里已按这个列表更新。

---

## 3. 还能不能用 Cursor 的 Composer Auto？

**可以，两者可以并存。**

| 选项 | 消耗 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
| **Cursor Auto / Auto Efficiency** | Cursor 订阅额度 | Composer 1.5、GPT-5.x 等，内置「智能与成本平衡」 |
| **OpenRouter openrouter/auto** | OpenRouter 余额 | 在 Claude、Step 3.5 Flash 等之间切换 |

在 Composer 里选模型时：
- 想用 Cursor 额度 → 选 **Auto**、**Premium** 等
- 想用 OpenRouter 额度 → 选你添加的 **openrouter/auto**

二者是不同模型入口，互不影响，按需切换即可。

---

## 逻辑关系

**Cursor 的 Auto 模式** 会在你**已启用的模型**里做选择。

| 状态 | 含义 |
|------|------|
| 启用（绿色） | 会被 Auto 纳入候选 |
| 禁用（灰色） | 不会被 Auto 选中 |

---

## 怎么用

1. 在 **Models** 里只开启你想要参与自动选择的模型
2. 在 Composer 里选择 **Auto** 或 **Auto Efficiency**
3. Cursor 会在这些启用的模型之间自动路由

例如：只启用 Composer 1.5、GPT-5.3 Codex、GPT-5.3 Codex Low，那 Auto 只会在这几个之间切换，不会用到你关掉的模型。

---

## 和 OpenRouter 的区别

| 设置位置 | 控制对象 |
|----------|----------|
| **Cursor Models 开关** | 控制 Cursor 内置模型（Composer、GPT-5.x 等）在 Auto 中的候选 |
| **OpenRouter Allowed Models** | 控制 `openrouter/auto` 时，在 OpenRouter 那边可选哪些模型 |

两套是独立的：Cursor 的开关只管 Cursor，OpenRouter 的配置只管 OpenRouter。
